01e2c05d067b3848.notes: No verifiable information about Epirus (the company) could be extracted from provided sources. Source [0] is a domain parking page with no company information. Source [1] is Wikipedia content about Epirus, a historical geographical region in the Balkans, not the defense technology company. Cannot verify any facts from the current database entry.

164c79678df6ca8d.name: Hermeus
164c79678df6ca8d.description: High-speed aircraft manufacturer focused on rapid design, build, and test of high-Mach and hypersonic aircraft for defense and national security missions. Develops the Quarterhorse unmanned testbed and Darkhorse hypersonic military jet.
164c79678df6ca8d.notes: Wikipedia (source 2) lists headquarters as Atlanta, Georgia. Company website and about page mention four locations: Atlanta, Los Angeles, Washington D.C., and Jacksonville, FL. Series C funding of $350M announced April 7, 2026 (source 1) led by Khosla Ventures and more than a dozen other investors; only Khosla Ventures explicitly named. Database entry references April 2026 HQ move to Los Angeles, but sources do not confirm this relocation. Valuation cannot be verified from sources provided.

22133c46096965ac.name: Radiant
22133c46096965ac.description: AI infrastructure platform providing integrated software, compute, and renewable energy capacity for large-scale AI deployment. Operates the world's largest powered-land portfolio with access to over 5 GW live and 45 GW of renewable generation capacity globally.
22133c46096965ac.notes: Database entry refers to nuclear microreactor company (Radiant), but sources provided describe AI infrastructure platform. These appear to be two different companies with the same name. No founders explicitly named in sources. No founded year, funding stage, or total raised amounts verified in provided sources.

96645ff7a1a27a93.name: Saildrone
96645ff7a1a27a93.description: Saildrone manufactures uncrewed surface vessels (USVs) powered by wind and solar energy for maritime domain awareness, undersea detection, ocean mapping, and defense missions. The company operates platforms including Spectre, Surveyor, Voyager, and Explorer across various applications from anti-submarine warfare to environmental sensing.
96645ff7a1a27a93.notes: Company expanded to Europe in 2025 with Copenhagen headquarters (Saildrone Denmark). Founded year not explicitly stated in sources despite database entry claiming 2012. Series stage and total funding amount not verified in provided sources. Investors list extracted from 'About' page statement: 'backed by BOND, XN, Standard Investments, Emerson Collective, Crowley Maritime Corporation, Capricorn's Technology Impact Fund, Lux Capital, Social Capital, Tribe Capital, The Schmidt Family Foundation, and Exor Seeds.'

6f1fd62ac2fee53f.notes: Source [2] is Wikipedia article about sieves (kitchen tools), not the company Sift. Source [0] describes Sift as a digital fraud prevention platform protecting 700+ brands with 1T+ annual events. Source [1] confirms 'Fighting Fraud Since 2011' and lists current leadership (Marc Friend as CEO) but does not name founders. Database entry lists founders 'Karthik Gollapudi, Austin Spiegel' and location 'El Segundo, CA' - these cannot be verified from provided sources. Database entry cites Series B funding and valuation - these cannot be verified from provided sources. No specific Series stage, total funding amount, or valuation mentioned in sources.

The sources provided do not match the company being fact-checked.

bc56498ca2459255.name: Boom Supersonic
bc56498ca2459255.description: American aerospace company developing the Overture, a proposed supersonic airliner designed to cruise at Mach 1.7. The company has built and flight-tested the XB-1 demonstrator, which broke the sound barrier on January 28, 2025.
bc56498ca2459255.notes: XB-1 became first privately funded aircraft to break the sound barrier on January 28, 2025, reaching Mach 1.122. Company has 130 orders/pre-orders from United Airlines, American Airlines, and Japan Airlines for Overture. Headquarters listed as Centennial, Colorado in Wikipedia; company founded in Denver, Colorado in 2014.

7fbda207c7349ace.notes: Source [0] is a LinkedIn page for an unrelated person (Joshua Schoen at Helix House). Source [1] is a Wikipedia article about cognition as a mental process, not about Cognition AI company. Neither source contains verifiable information about Cognition AI company. Cannot verify any facts from provided sources.

31744ffcacecef13.name: Scale AI
31744ffcacecef13.description: An artificial intelligence infrastructure and software company providing data annotation, RLHF services, LLM evaluation, and enterprise software suites to build and deploy AI applications.
31744ffcacecef13.notes: In June 2025, Meta Platforms acquired a 49% non-voting stake for $14.8 billion. Founder Alexandr Wang departed to join Meta and was replaced as CEO by Jason Droege. The database entry listed Stage as 'Series A' which cannot be verified from sources; company is structured as a private entity with major institutional investment.

c3fc770df5b290a0.name: Bedrock Robotics
c3fc770df5b290a0.description: Develops autonomous systems for heavy construction equipment, enabling fully autonomous operations on job sites. Founded by engineers from Waymo who previously worked on autonomous vehicle systems.
c3fc770df5b290a0.notes: Founder names not explicitly listed in sources provided. Database entry mentions 'Boris Sofman' as founder but this is not verified in the provided sources. Series B funding of $270M mentioned in NYT reference in source [1]. Database entry claims $350M+ total raised and $1.75B valuation but these figures are not found in provided sources.

1560b2f35ab8614b.name: Terra Industries
1560b2f35ab8614b.description: Autonomous systems company building AI-powered drones and ground vehicles for critical infrastructure protection in Africa. Produces surveillance drones (Archer VTOL, Iroko UAV), autonomous ground vehicles (Duma UGV), and AI-powered sentries (Kallon).
1560b2f35ab8614b.notes: Company operates as 'Terra Industries' on website, not 'Terrahaptix'. Database entry references 'dba Terra Industries' which aligns with source. Website footer shows copyright 2026. No founder names, investor names, founding year, current funding stage, or valuation data found in provided sources. Database claims about NetHawk contract, 8VC + Lux Capital investors, 8 African country exports, and 2024 founding year could not be verified from website source alone.

e3b90ff8dd0b2699.name: Bear Robotics
e3b90ff8dd0b2699.description: Autonomous mobile robots for hospitality and logistics. Bear Robotics produces service robots including the Servi and Servi Plus models for restaurants and hotels.
e3b90ff8dd0b2699.notes: Founded May 2017. Series C funding of $60M from LG Electronics announced 2024. Series B of $81M led by IMM in 2022. Series A of $32M with SoftBank Robotics in 2020. Seed of $2.8M in 2018. Angel round of $750K in 2017. Total calculated: $750K + $2.8M + $32M + $81M + $60M = $175.8M. 2025 partnership with LG Electronics announced for broader robotics applications beyond hospitality.

e6672f298a881ff7.name: Saronic Technologies
e6672f298a881ff7.description: Defense technology company that develops autonomous surface vessels for maritime operations. The company provides advanced autonomous vessels with capabilities including navigation, perception, communications, and payload integration.
e6672f298a881ff7.notes: Source [2] is about Saronic Gulf (geographic location in Greece), not the company, so it was not used. Sources [0] and [1] confirm the company exists and describe its business, but do not contain founding year, total raised, current valuation, or current funding stage. Location (Austin, TX) from database entry could not be verified in provided sources. Founders explicitly named as co-founders in source [1]: Dino Mavrookas (Co-Founder and CEO), Rob Lehman (Co-Founder and CCO), Vibhav Altekar (Co-Founder and CTO), Doug Lambert (Co-Founder and COO).

8df950716c6a183f.name: Figure AI
8df950716c6a183f.description: American robotics company developing humanoid robots controlled by artificial intelligence. The company has developed three generations of humanoid robots (Figure 01-03) and vision-language-action models for industrial and household applications.
8df950716c6a183f.notes: Source 0 is a domain sales page and contains no information about Figure AI the company. All verified information comes from Wikipedia (source 1). Series C funding exceeded $1 billion in September 2025, raising valuation to $39 billion.

54ece707b443c90b.name: Skild AI
54ece707b443c90b.notes: Source [0] is company website with minimal content (launching soon page only). No factual claims about founders, location, founding year, funding, valuation, or business description could be verified from provided sources.

40641daeef67fe73.name: Fortastra
40641daeef67fe73.description: Builds intelligent spacecraft designed for active defense and maneuvering to protect space infrastructure and satellite systems.
40641daeef67fe73.notes: Company announced December 11, 2025 per source [1]. Database entry claims Series C stage and $8M seed funding, but these cannot be verified from provided sources. Founder name 'Mike Smayda' and location 'Torrance, CA' also cannot be verified from provided sources. News article from April 2, 2026 mentions executives joining from Relativity, Hermeus, Astrion and Divergent but does not name them as founders or provide funding details.

c5395e87d152e314.name: Varda Space Industries
c5395e87d152e314.description: American space manufacturing company that designs, builds, and flies spacecraft to process pharmaceuticals in microgravity and return them to Earth via reentry capsules. The company focuses on small molecule crystallization and brings crystals back to Earth with atmospheric reentry vehicles.
c5395e87d152e314.notes: Founded in January 2021 (not 2020 as in database entry). Wikipedia cites $42M Series A (July 2021) + $90M Series B (April 2024) = $132M total, not $187M. No Series C funding found in sources. Database entry claims 5 missions in 2025; Wikipedia documents W-1 (June 2023), W-2 (January 2025), W-3 (March 2025), W-4 (June 2025) — only 4 documented. Valuation of $1.5B not found in sources.

b54b7be89900aa86.notes: Sources provided do not contain information about the aerospace/defense manufacturing company Hadrian described in the database entry. Source [0] describes 'The Hadrian Corporation' as a HIPAA-compliant telephone answering and call management service company. Source [1] is about the Roman emperor Hadrian. Neither source matches the frontier-tech manufacturing company referenced in the database entry. No verifiable facts could be extracted.

358911c73614413c.notes: Source [0] is a domain sale listing page for graymatterrobotics.com, not company information. No verifiable facts about GrayMatter Robotics could be extracted from provided sources.

a20c6d6e8da5b571.name: Multiply Labs
a20c6d6e8da5b571.description: Robotic biomanufacturing platform using physical AI and robotics to automate cell and gene therapy production. The company's Robotic Cluster automates existing GMP instruments in closed environments to increase throughput and reduce costs.
a20c6d6e8da5b571.notes: Company website and team page do not contain founding year, funding stage, total raised, or valuation information. Database entry claims 2016 founding and Series A stage with $41M+ raised, but these cannot be verified from provided sources. Announced collaborations include AstraZeneca, Legend Biotech, and Kyverna Therapeutics per source [0].

7d94f4155467fb75.notes: Both provided sources are domain sale pages from HugeDomains, not actual company sources. The domain orangewoodlabs.com is for sale and does not contain company information. No verifiable facts about Orangewood Labs could be extracted from these sources.

90d4c5203839ff47.name: Cohere
90d4c5203839ff47.description: Canadian artificial intelligence company specializing in large language models and AI products for regulated industries including finance, healthcare, manufacturing, energy, and public sector. Founded in 2019 by Google Brain researchers including a co-author of the transformer architecture paper.
90d4c5203839ff47.notes: Wikipedia source mentions Series A ($40M, September 2021), Series B ($125M, February 2022), and Series C ($270M, June 2023) but the article text is truncated. In April 2026, Cohere agreed to acquire Aleph Alpha. Source [1] states 'nearly $1 billion' raised across four major rounds (Series A through D) between 2021-2024, but exact total and current stage cannot be verified with precision. Current stage is listed as private but not formally stated as Series D or other specific round in complete sources.

7ebb5473102366dc.name: Castelion
7ebb5473102366dc.description: Castelion designs, tests, and manufactures hypersonic strike weapons and related military systems. The company combines rapid iteration, vertical integration, and scalable manufacturing to deliver affordable, high-performance systems for the U.S. Department of Defense.
7ebb5473102366dc.notes: Database entry listed Series B with $350M raise led by Altimeter and Lightspeed, but source [1] indicates Series A in January 2025 for $100M (including $70M Series A and $30M venture debt from Silicon Valley Bank) led by Lightspeed Venture Partners. The database entry appears to contain inaccurate or outdated information. No founded year is explicitly stated in sources; 2023 is implied by October 2023 seed announcement but not explicitly stated as founding date.

34da3b48cdbb5b73.notes: Sources provided do not contain information about Pano AI. Both sources [0] and [1] appear to be pages for 'Gradial', a different company focused on agentic marketing operations. No verifiable facts about Pano AI could be extracted from these sources.

4ca83383ce164d34.name: ATMOS Space Cargo
4ca83383ce164d34.description: European space company developing reusable spacecraft and return logistics for controlled access to orbit and Earth return. Operates the PHOENIX orbital transfer and return vehicle (OTRV) enabling routine LEO access, autonomous orbital missions, and rapid payload recovery.
4ca83383ce164d34.notes: Four co-founders identified (Klaus, Oliveira, Hendrikse, Grimm). Source [1] lists offices in Lichtenau and Strasbourg; primary location cited as Lichtenau. Founded year 2021 from database entry could not be verified from provided sources. Total raised ~$14M and Series A stage from database could not be verified from provided sources. Sources list 70+ employees and 7 launches planned for 2026-2028.

be40c3ff2f88946e.name: Shield AI
be40c3ff2f88946e.description: American aerospace and defense technology company that develops artificial intelligence-powered drones and autonomous systems for military operations. The company's products include Nova (autonomous quadcopter), V-BAT (vertical takeoff and landing aircraft), and Hivemind autonomy software.
be40c3ff2f88946e.notes: CRITICAL: Sources [0] and [1] describe a different Shield AI company founded in 2024 that provides GenAI data protection/compliance software. Wikipedia [2] describes the correct Shield AI - a defense technology company founded in 2015. The database entry refers to the defense company. Gary Steele became CEO in March 2025 (replacing Ryan Tseng, who became Chief Strategic Officer). Series G funding announced March 2026 at $12.7B post-money valuation with $1.5B raise.

cdea649aeefd8fb4.name: Turion Space
cdea649aeefd8fb4.description: Builds autonomous spacecraft and software infrastructure for space operations, including satellites, propulsion systems, and a unified command platform called StarfireOS for mission planning and fleet management.
cdea649aeefd8fb4.notes: Founded year not explicitly stated in sources. Database entry lists 2020, but this cannot be verified from provided sources. Location identified as 'Southern California' in source [1]; Irvine, CA from database entry cannot be verified. Series B funding of $75M+ confirmed in press releases dated 4/15/26. Company acquired Tychee Research Group as of 1/13/26.

c6406eea8c482a7c.name: Axiom Space
c6406eea8c482a7c.description: Private aerospace company providing human spaceflight services to the International Space Station and developing Axiom Station, a modular commercial space station. The company also develops next-generation spacesuits for low-Earth orbit and beyond.
c6406eea8c482a7c.notes: Source [0] mentions $350M financing in February 2026, but this is a news headline only and not independently verified with full context in the provided sources. Wikipedia source [2] confirms 4iG Group $100 million equity investment announced December 2025. Michael Suffredini served as first CEO and President until 2024; Jonathan W. Cirtain is current CEO as of source [1].

19b17ca46c349620.name: Aetherflux
19b17ca46c349620.description: American renewable energy company developing space-based solar power using low Earth orbit satellites that transmit energy to ground stations through infrared lasers. Initial applications target artificial intelligence compute in orbit and power delivery to military and remote operations.
19b17ca46c349620.notes: Database entry incorrectly listed stage as 'Series B'; Wikipedia confirms Series A funding of $50M announced April 2025, bringing total raised to $60M. Database entry did not list any investors; multiple investors identified in Wikipedia source. First space demonstration planned for early 2026; orbital data center ('Galactic Brain') targeted for Q1 2027.

70f43390ac8fee4b.name: Built Robotics
70f43390ac8fee4b.description: A San Francisco-based startup that develops software and hardware to automate construction equipment, including an aftermarket kit called the Exosystem that adds autonomous robotic capabilities to heavy equipment through GPS, cameras, and artificial intelligence.
70f43390ac8fee4b.notes: Wikipedia states total raised is $112M across Series A, B, and C. Database entry lists $137M which cannot be verified from these sources. Company acquired Roin Technologies in 2023.

e56b2f01aedce2d9.name: EnduroSat
e56b2f01aedce2d9.description: Satellite manufacturer and space service provider that designs nanosatellites and offers space data services. The company provides flight-proven satellites, avionics modules, and a space service marketplace to enable access to space intelligence.
e56b2f01aedce2d9.notes: Founder Raycho Raychev explicitly identified as 'Founder & CEO' in source [1]. Company founded in 2015 in an attic apartment per timeline in source [1]. Website sources show company has 80 satellites in orbit, 3500 modules in orbit, 500 TB data downlinked, and 400 customers as of 2025. Current stage and total raised could not be verified from provided sources.

660bedab9a227145.name: Ares Industries
660bedab9a227145.description: Designs and manufactures precision-guided munitions and missile systems including Maverick and Goose platforms intended for mass production and rapid deployment.
660bedab9a227145.notes: Source 0 (company website) does not contain founder names, location, founding year, funding stage, total raised, valuation, or investor information. Database entry references cannot be used as verification sources per instructions.

Craig Beddis identified as CEO in source [1] and joined Hadean 'in early 2017' but no explicit founding year is stated. d6db8fb0ebd69ad7.name: Blykalla

d6db8fb0ebd69ad7.notes: Database entry mentions Series B stage and $60M+ raised, but these claims cannot be verified from the provided sources. Sources indicate offices in Stockholm and New York. SEALER-55 specified as 55 MW output reactor.

42d931fc4de74d16.notes: Source [0] is a domain for-sale listing page with no company information. No verifiable facts about Harmattan AI could be extracted from provided sources. Database entry claims cannot be verified.

a36bc1990c2bdd26.name: Anysphere
a36bc1990c2bdd26.description: An American software company offering Cursor, an AI-assisted code editor that integrates generative models from multiple providers to assist with software development.
a36bc1990c2bdd26.notes: Series D closed November 13, 2025 for $2.3B at $29.3B valuation. Company surpassed $1B ARR by November 2025. Co-founder Arvid Lunnemark departed in October 2025. As of April 2026, xAI announced an acquisition deal for $60B pending later in 2026.

67b78d6cb8bbc4e1.name: Infleqtion
67b78d6cb8bbc4e1.description: Global leader in quantum technology delivering neutral atom solutions for quantum computing, sensing, and security. The company develops quantum computers, optical atomic clocks, RF receivers, and inertial sensors with systems in use by the U.S. Department of War, NASA, and the U.K. government.
67b78d6cb8bbc4e1.notes: Company is listed on NYSE under ticker INFQ (source 1: 'Infleqtion, Inc. (NYSE: INFQ)'). Headquarters listed as Louisville, CO at 1315 West Century Drive, Suite 150. Dana Anderson identified as Founder and Chief Science Officer; Pranav Gokhale identified as Co-founder and Chief Technology Officer in leadership section.

d7fbe4502f1dfe71.notes: Source [0] is a domain listing page (quantumart.io for sale on Spaceship.com) with no company information. Cannot verify any facts about Quantum Art from provided sources. Database entry cannot be validated.

002fd3ed761be7da.name: Aleph Farms
002fd3ed761be7da.description: Cellular agriculture company that grows cultivated beef steaks from cow cells using tissue engineering and 3D bioprinting technology. Received first regulatory approval from Israeli Ministry of Health in January 2024 to sell lab-grown steaks in Israel.
002fd3ed761be7da.notes: Wikipedia source [2] mentions Series B funding of $105 million in July 2021. Third co-founder Dr. Neta Lavon is listed as Chief Technology Officer in source [1] but not explicitly named as co-founder in founder section. Database entry mentions June 2024 layoffs and $25M emergency raise at $90M valuation, but these specific claims are not verifiable in provided sources.

07a66cd51ed459b1.name: Apptronik
07a66cd51ed459b1.description: Apptronik develops Apollo, a general purpose humanoid robot designed for logistics, manufacturing, and retail tasks such as case picking, palletizing, and trailer unloading.
07a66cd51ed459b1.notes: Only Jeff Cardenas is explicitly named as founder/CEO in sources; Nick Paine not confirmed as founder in provided sources. The company in these sources is a SaaS decision-making platform. This appears to be a different company entirely, or a significant mismatch between the database entry and provided sources.

8715096fa1f1cf93.name: Axelera AI

8c74e5153e44138c.name: Helical Fusion
8c74e5153e44138c.description: A stellarator fusion company developing commercial fusion reactors and related technologies, spun out from the National Institute for Fusion Science in 2021.
8c74e5153e44138c.notes: Series B 1st close of approximately ¥2.7B (~$27M) announced. Cumulative funding (累計調達額) stated as ¥98B (~$98M) as of Series B announcement in source [0]. Founder names extracted from database entry but could not be independently verified in provided sources. Japan's first fusion energy power purchase agreement (電力売買契約) signed in December 2025.

No verifiable company information (name, description, founders, location, funding, stage) could be extracted from the provided source content. The source does not contain factual claims about the company that can be verified.

9b9fefd07f2d38b7.name: Teramount
9b9fefd07f2d38b7.description: Teramount develops optical fiber connectivity solutions using a patented Universal Photonic Coupler for connecting fibers to silicon photonics chips. The technology enables high-volume manufacturing of co-packaged optics for data centers and advanced computing applications.
9b9fefd07f2d38b7.notes: Teramount was acquired by Molex Incorporated as announced on 15 April, 2026 per source [0]. The database entry references a $50M Series B round in 2025, but this could not be verified in the provided sources. Co-founder name in database listed as 'Abraham Israel' but source [1] identifies him as 'Avi Israel' (full name Avi Israel, PhD).

3343a7c158fdf974.name: Monumo
3343a7c158fdf974.description: A deeptech company using Engineering AI to optimize complex engineering systems, starting with electric motors and drives for EV powertrains.
3343a7c158fdf974.notes: Sources list Martin Frost as 'Chair & Investor, Founder' and Ian Murphy as 'President, Founder'. Jarek Rzepecki is listed as current CEO. The database entry lists Dominic Vergine as founder, but this person is not mentioned in provided sources. Location (Cambridge, UK), founded year (2021), stage (Series B), and total_raised ($100M) from database entry cannot be verified from these sources.

dbb188fafada592f.name: DustPhotonics
dbb188fafada592f.description: Silicon photonics company developing co-packaged optics for 800Gb/s and 1.6Tb/s optical interconnects in data centers.
dbb188fafada592f.notes: Company website explicitly states 'Credo to acquire DustPhotonics' — the company has been acquired and is no longer independent. Previous database entry listed Series C stage is now outdated. No founder names, location, founding year, or fundraising details found in provided source.

8c7ea6b4b916247d.name: Vention
8c7ea6b4b916247d.description: Manufacturing automation platform combining engineering software and plug-and-play automation components for designing, programming, and deploying automated equipment and robot cells. The platform serves manufacturers across 25+ industries with over 25,000 machines deployed in 4,000+ factories.
8c7ea6b4b916247d.notes: Series C closed in 2022 for over $95M USD led by Georgian and Fidelity (source 2). Wikipedia lists website as https://vention.io while company website header shows https://vention.com; both are verified sources. Company has expanded with offices in Berlin (2021) and Boston (2022), and opened Montreal distribution center in 2023.

35825c7f9fd3c634.name: Lightelligence
35825c7f9fd3c634.description: Photonic integrated circuits company focused on optical AI accelerators and photonic computing chips. Founded in 2017 and based in Boston and Shanghai, the company develops photonic processors for AI workloads.
35825c7f9fd3c634.notes: Company held IPO on Hong Kong Stock Exchange (SEHK: 1879) on 28 April 2026. Traded as Shanghai Xizhi Technology Co., Ltd. The database entry references $270M+ raised, but this figure is not explicitly stated in the Wikipedia source provided.

980e5ca5f2d6c75b.name: Thea Energy
980e5ca5f2d6c75b.description: American fusion power company using planar-coil stellarator technology with high-temperature superconducting magnets, licensed from Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory. The company is building Eos, a neutron source demonstrator based on the same technology.
980e5ca5f2d6c75b.notes: Company formerly known as Princeton Stellarators, Inc. (PSI). Wikipedia source [2] is the only source providing verifiable information about the fusion company. Sources [0] and [1] describe a different company (Thea Energy Pvt. Ltd., an Indian solar inverter manufacturer), which is not the same entity as the fusion company. The database entry references a $37M+ Series A and $20M DOE milestone award; Wikipedia confirms only the $20M Series A funding (September 2024) but does not mention a $37M figure or specify it as DOE milestone funding.

6c02a0f03b940647.name: Realta Fusion
6c02a0f03b940647.description: Fusion energy company developing compact, scalable, modular fusion systems using magnetic mirror technology to decarbonize industrial process heat and electricity.
6c02a0f03b940647.notes: Five co-founders identified in source [1]: Kieran Furlong (CEO & Co-Founder), Cary Forest (CSO & Co-Founder), Jay Anderson (Co-Founder), Ben Lindley (Co-Founder), and Oliver Schmitz (Co-Founder). Database entry lists only two founders and includes unverified information about Madison, WI location, 2022 founding year, Series A stage, and $36M+ funding—none of which appear in provided sources.

6ef9fd74b90f082f.name: Elementl Power
6ef9fd74b90f082f.description: Nuclear project developer meeting America's long-term energy demands by developing safe, affordable, next-generation nuclear plants at utility scale. The company combines a technology agnostic approach with expertise in capital formation, project execution, and regulatory strategy.
6ef9fd74b90f082f.notes: Website mentions CEO Chris Colbert as 'former senior executive at NuScale Power' and that team was 'Founded by U.S. Navy nuclear engineers and energy finance professionals.' Website confirms strategic partnership with Google in 2025 but does not specify deal size. Database entry claims $50M+ raised, Series A stage, and 1.8 GW deal—none of these are mentioned in the provided source. Energy Impact Partners described as '$4+ billion energy investment firm' but company's own funding stage/amount not disclosed in source.

371d2535951d79f3.name: Orbital Composites
371d2535951d79f3.description: Orbital builds autonomous robotic systems that 3D print high-performance composite parts for defense, aerospace, and space applications. The company develops robotic production cells and closed-loop physical AI designed to produce composite parts at scale with 75% less labor.
371d2535951d79f3.notes: Source [0] confirms 75% labor reduction claim from database entry. Source [0] mentions funding from U.S. Department of Defense and Department of Energy but does not provide specific amounts or founding year. Founder name Cole Nielsen and location Campbell, CA from database entry are not verified in provided sources. Current stage (Seed) and total_raised ($6.6M+) from database entry are not verified in provided sources.

3536200f12f3267f.name: Astranis
3536200f12f3267f.description: American satellite company specializing in small, radiation-hardened geostationary communications satellites for high orbits. Astranis builds dedicated satellite networks for government and commercial customers.
3536200f12f3267f.notes: Founded October 20, 2015. Part of Y Combinator Winter 2016 cohort. First MicroGEO satellite (Arcturus) launched April 30, 2023. As of Wikipedia source, company is private with 250+ employees (2023). Current stage not explicitly stated in sources.

73df39433f4e63ee.name: Watershed
73df39433f4e63ee.description: Enterprise sustainability platform that helps companies measure, reduce, and report their carbon emissions using AI-powered data tools and industry-leading climate databases.
73df39433f4e63ee.notes: Source [1] explicitly names only Taylor Francis as co-founder in the interview reference; Christian Anderson and Avi Itskovich from database entry are not mentioned in provided sources. Series C announced February 2024 per source [1]. Advisors include Christiana Figueres and Al Gore per source [1].

b5f0423a9848ee2a.name: Cellares
b5f0423a9848ee2a.description: An Integrated Development and Manufacturing Organization (IDMO) that develops and operates automated technologies for cell therapy manufacturing, including the Cell Shuttle platform for cGMP manufacturing and Cell Q for quality control.
b5f0423a9848ee2a.notes: Sources confirm Fabian Gerlinghaus and Omar Kurdi as co-founders. Database entry lists Franziska Blaeschke and Alex Kolicich as founders, but these individuals are not mentioned in provided sources. Founded year (2019) and total_raised ($350M+) from database entry cannot be verified from provided sources. Current stage cannot be verified from provided sources.

ed40942ca629a1a0.name: Nyobolt
ed40942ca629a1a0.description: UK-based energy storage technology company that develops high-power lithium-ion batteries with niobium-based anodes designed for ultrafast charging. The company's batteries are capable of charging to 80% in under 5 minutes and are tailored for applications including electric vehicles, robotics, and industrial automation.
ed40942ca629a1a0.notes: Sources confirm ultra-fast charging capability (0-80% in under 5 minutes) and niobium-based anode technology. Database entry mentions '$150M contracted backlog' and 'Series B' stage, but these specific details are not verifiable in provided sources. Sources reference Series A and B funding rounds but do not specify total amount raised or current stage.

4ae243909e30b171.name: Hystar
4ae243909e30b171.description: High-tech company specializing in ultra-efficient PEM electrolysers for the production of green hydrogen from water electrolysis. Hystar's market-leading PEM stacks have the world's highest efficiency and are made in Høvik, Norway.
4ae243909e30b171.notes: Most recent funding announcement from May 13, 2025 indicates $36M raised. Earlier database entry referenced $62M+ equity + €26M EU Innovation Fund, but this could not be verified in provided sources. Founders not explicitly named in sources provided. Founded year 2020 from database entry could not be verified in sources.

308b062572406513.name: QunaSys
308b062572406513.description: Quantum computer algorithm research and software development company focused on quantum computing applications in industry. Provides algorithms and software to maximize quantum computer performance for practical business solutions.
308b062572406513.notes: Database entry references Series B2 funding of JPY 1.7B in late 2024 and $33M total raised, but these figures could not be verified from provided sources. Founder name in database listed as 'Tennin Yan' matches source [1] which lists '楊　天任' (Yang Tianren/Tennin Yan) as 代表取締役 (Representative Director). Company has offices in both Tokyo and Copenhagen.

f9bfc8c225cd9002.notes: Source [0] is a domain marketplace listing page for classiq.com (Atom domain sales platform), not the actual Classiq company website or any information about Classiq the quantum computing company. No verifiable information about Classiq Technologies could be extracted from the provided sources.

535b4b6212c300ef.name: Neustark
535b4b6212c300ef.description: Neustark permanently stores CO2 from the atmosphere in recycled mineral waste such as demolished concrete, slag, or IBA through mineralization technology. The company operates 47 capture and storage sites across Switzerland and Europe.
535b4b6212c300ef.notes: Company website does not explicitly list founders by name, founding year, funding stage, total raised, or investors. The database entry references cannot be verified from these sources. Website confirms 47 operating sites and describes permanent CO2 mineralization in recycled concrete.

0200bdbda60fdd48.name: Porotech
0200bdbda60fdd48.description: A spin-out from the University of Cambridge developing porous-GaN (PoroGaN®) semiconductor materials and Dynamic Pixel Tuning® technology for microLED displays. The company enables single microLED chips to produce any visible color from a single pixel for applications including AR/VR displays.
0200bdbda60fdd48.notes: Company officially moved out of University of Cambridge after first equity investment in January 2020. Currently operates across three locations: Cambridge headquarters (UK), Hsinchu (Taiwan), and Arizona (USA). Database entry lists 'Series A' stage and '$20M+' raised, but these values could not be verified from provided sources.

52d20376ddb4f32a.name: Isembard
52d20376ddb4f32a.description: A distributed manufacturing company that scales production from prototype to factory-level output, powered by proprietary AI software called MasonOS for precision machining in aerospace, defense, and robotics.
52d20376ddb4f32a.notes: Database entry lists $50M raised and Series A stage, but sources provided (company website and about page) do not contain specific funding amount or stage information. Headline references mention '$9M Seed Round' and '£7m boost' but these are not fully visible in the provided source text. Founded year listed as 2024 in database but not verified in provided sources. Alexander Fitzgerald is listed as CEO in source [1], explicitly named as founder/CEO.

be696e5580963bfd.name: Perigee Aerospace
be696e5580963bfd.description: South Korean private developer and manufacturer of orbital and sub-orbital launch vehicles. The company develops the small orbital rocket Blue Whale 1, capable of delivering up to 170 kg to a Sun-synchronous orbit.
be696e5580963bfd.notes: Wikipedia lists 'Yoon Shin' as CEO; founder name from reference citation is 'Shin Dong-yoon'. Company began work in 2012 with sounding rockets but was formally established in 2018. No sources provided verify Series D stage or $56.64M total raised figures from database entry. Database entry mentions Esrange spaceport (Sweden) and Philippine Space Agency contracts, but these are not mentioned in provided sources.

f1a6e972dce97443.notes: The provided sources do not contain information about Hailo Technologies (the AI chip company referenced in the database entry). Source [0] is the company website for Hailo.ai (AI processors) but contains no extractable structured data about founders, founding year, funding, or location. Source [1] is Wikipedia documentation of Hailo (a defunct British taxi app founded 2011), which is a completely different company. No verifiable facts can be extracted for the AI chip company Hailo from these sources.

68e860ddb9a0bcc3.name: NordSpace
68e860ddb9a0bcc3.description: Canadian aerospace company developing small orbital and suborbital launch vehicles (Tundra and Taiga), 3D-printed liquid rocket engines, and the Atlantic Spaceport Complex in Newfoundland and Labrador.
68e860ddb9a0bcc3.notes: Database entry mentions '$38M+ raised' and 'Series A' stage, but no sources provided support these claims. Wikipedia notes Goel invested approximately CA$5,000,000 of personal capital (source 2), but this is founder investment, not total funding raised. No investor names or funding rounds are mentioned in provided sources. Taiga suborbital launch campaign occurred in August 2025 with no successful launch; database reference to 'maiden suborbital launch planned winter 2026' cannot be verified from these sources.

063d076dcb599069.name: Corintis
063d076dcb599069.description: Provider of microfluidic chip cooling solutions for data centers and high-performance computing. The company designs and manufactures liquid cooling systems for high-power chips, claiming up to 10× better cooling performance than conventional methods.
063d076dcb599069.notes: Most recent funding mentioned is '25M USD Series A1 funding round successfully led by Applied Digital' from source 0. Database entry lists $24M total raised and Series A; source specifies Series A1 round of $25M. Founders listed in database (Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, Elison Matioli) cannot be verified from provided sources. Founded year 2020 in database cannot be verified from sources.

c5a013d525043fc4.notes: CRITICAL MISMATCH: Sources provided are for Asterra, a real estate brokerage firm in Austin, TX established in 2008 by Lucian Morehead and Andrew Karr. The database entry describes a satellite-based subsurface leak detection company founded in 2016 in Herzliya, Israel by Lauren Guy. These are two entirely different companies. The provided sources contain NO information about the frontier-tech company described in the database entry. Cannot verify any fields for the tech company.

eca786814f270010.name: Portal Space Systems
eca786814f270010.description: American private aerospace company that develops maneuverable spacecraft for orbital mobility using solar thermal propulsion technology. The company manufactures Supernova and Starburst spacecraft platforms designed for defense, civil, and commercial missions.
eca786814f270010.notes: Total raised calculated from verified funding rounds: $3M (April 2024 DoD), $17.5M (2025 seed round led by AlleyCorp), and $45M (December 2025 STRATFI from U.S. Space Force), plus $350K grant from State of Washington (non-equity). Company has three co-founders, not two. No Series A designation found in source; current stage cannot be verified.

e710a8575ed645ea.name: Crusoe
e710a8575ed645ea.description: AI infrastructure company that designs, builds, and operates data centers and cloud platforms powered by renewable and alternative energy sources.
e710a8575ed645ea.notes: Sources do not contain founder names, location, founding year, current stage, or investor names. October 24, 2025 news states 'raising $1.375 billion at a valuation above $10 billion'. Database entry claims include bitcoin mining history and Stargate project involvement, which are not found in provided sources.

06e05ec33b062da3.name: Field AI
06e05ec33b062da3.description: General-purpose embodied AI foundation models for robots in complex industrial environments. Built by veterans from DeepMind, NASA JPL, Tesla, NVIDIA, Amazon, and beyond.
06e05ec33b062da3.notes: Ali Agha and Shayegan Omidshafiei are identified as leaders and co-founders based on their prominent leadership roles and history of working together at MIT and NASA JPL. David Fan, Sebastian Scherer, and Eric Krotrov are identified as team members/leaders but not explicitly named as founders. No verified information found for: location (Irvine, CA claimed in database entry but not in sources), founded year (2023 claimed but not verified), Series A stage, $405M raised, or $2B valuation.

bee294bb539cdc6a.name: Agility Robotics
bee294bb539cdc6a.description: American humanoid robotics company that manufactures Digit, a bipedal robot designed for warehouse and logistics automation. Digit has been deployed in commercial operations at facilities including Amazon and GXO Logistics.
bee294bb539cdc6a.notes: Company rebranded as 'Agility' on March 5, 2026 per Wikipedia source [2]. Digit became first FCC approved and NRTL certified humanoid robot in 2025 per source [1]. Manufacturing facility (RoboFab) located in Salem, OR with peak capacity of 10,000 robots annually per source [1].

69ba534ef5d58ca7.notes: Source [0] is a domain sale listing page (Spaceship.com) for hiveai.io, not actual company information. No verifiable facts about Hive AI could be extracted from provided sources.

6f6e47775a5d8c9d.name: QuEra Computing
6f6e47775a5d8c9d.description: Quantum computing company developing neutral-atom quantum computers based on rubidium atoms controlled by lasers. Founded by Harvard and MIT scientists, the company commercializes technology from Mikhail Lukin's Harvard lab.
6f6e47775a5d8c9d.notes: Source [0] states 'QuEra Computing Completes $230M Financing to Accelerate Development of Large-Scale Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computers' (February 11, 2025) and 'QuEra Computing Marks Record 2025 as the Year of Fault Tolerance and Over $230M of New Capital to Accelerate Industrial Deployment' (December 9, 2025). Current stage not specified in sources. Valuation not found. Specific investor names not listed in sources. Database entry lists only three founders but Wikipedia and company sources identify six co-founders including Dirk Englund and Nathan Gemelke.

5ff5ef9ecd252ae3.name: Pulsar Fusion
5ff5ef9ecd252ae3.description: UK-based aerospace company developing clean space propulsion systems including electric thrusters and a nuclear fusion-powered rocket engine called Sunbird for interplanetary propulsion.
5ff5ef9ecd252ae3.notes: Wikipedia article contains a conflicting founding date categorization ('established in 2019') but the article text and all other sources consistently cite 2011. Source [0] website mentions 'IOD testing in 2027' for Sunbird but no March 2026 plasma ignition event is verifiable from these sources. CEO title confirmed in source [1] as 'FOUNDER & CEO'.

adea0514ff6010cd.name: Zama
adea0514ff6010cd.description: Open source cryptography company building Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) solutions for blockchain enabling computation on encrypted data.
adea0514ff6010cd.notes: Sources confirm company name, website, and general FHE/blockchain focus. Database entry claims Paris location, 2020 founding, Series B $57M (June 2025), and founders Rand Hindi and Pascal Paillier, but these specifics cannot be verified from provided sources. Founder names explicitly not found in sources. No financial or founding year information present in sources.

e87609dcc476eba1.name: Genespire
e87609dcc476eba1.description: Develops off-the-shelf in-vivo gene therapies using proprietary immune-shielded lentiviral vectors (ISLV) designed for one-time treatment of genetic diseases in adults and children.
e87609dcc476eba1.notes: Database entry claims Series B $52M led by Sofinnova and founded 2020, but these details are not supported by provided sources. Sources only confirm company name, location, founders, description of technology, and website.

6448547dc4e7ae88.name: QuamCore
6448547dc4e7ae88.description: Develops superconducting quantum processor architecture designed for massive scaling with fault-tolerant capabilities targeting millions of qubits.
6448547dc4e7ae88.notes: Source [0] lists Alon Cohen as CEO but does not explicitly identify him as a founder/co-founder. Yonatan Cohen mentioned in database entry is not identified in provided sources. No founding year, funding details, location, or investor information found in sources. Prof. Shay Hacohen-Gourgy, Prof. Serge Rosenblum, and Prof. Eby G. Friedman are listed as CTO, Chief Scientist, and Scientific Founding Member respectively, but database entry does not list them as founders.

df8974268457add1.name: Quantum Motion
df8974268457add1.description: Silicon-based quantum computing company developing scalable quantum processors using standard CMOS silicon processing technology compatible with existing semiconductor manufacturing.
df8974268457add1.notes: Database entry lists founded year 2017 and Series A stage with $60M+ raised, but these claims cannot be verified from provided sources. Sources confirm silicon-based quantum computing architecture and offices in London, UK and San Sebastian, Spain. John Morton listed as Founder, CTO and Simon Benjamin as Founder, CSO in team listing.

ced8e166bba6aabe.name: Causaly
ced8e166bba6aabe.description: AI platform for enterprise-scale scientific research in life sciences that helps scientists find, visualize, and interpret biomedical knowledge and automate research workflows. The platform uses agentic AI agents and a scientific knowledge graph to accelerate drug discovery and R&D decision-making.
ced8e166bba6aabe.notes: Database entry claims $95M raised in Series B led by ICONIQ and lists major pharma deployments, but these details cannot be verified from provided sources. Sources confirm headquarters in London with offices in New York and Greece.

cc8f396b612fe28d.name: Black Forest Labs
cc8f396b612fe28d.description: Frontier AI research lab for visual intelligence based in Freiburg, Germany, that develops FLUX text-to-image generation models.
cc8f396b612fe28d.notes: Database entry listed $200M+ total raised, but company website announcement from December 1, 2025 states '$300M Series B'. Initial investment of $31M mentioned in Wikipedia source [2].

a688400007b5c022.notes: Source [0] is Wikipedia article about dust (the substance), not the company Dust. No information about the company Dust can be verified from the provided sources.

91a67463def1133f.name: Photoroom
91a67463def1133f.description: AI-powered photo editor and visual solution for e-commerce that removes backgrounds, generates product images, and creates listing-ready visuals at scale.
91a67463def1133f.notes: Co-founder identified as 'Matt' (Matthieu Rouif) in about page narrative. Database entry references Series B $43M at $500M valuation December 2024, but these figures are not present in provided sources and therefore marked null per accuracy rules.

7dad7e3bc0c15d28.name: MakinaRocks
7dad7e3bc0c15d28.description: Physical AI company that develops enterprise AI OS and specialized AI for industrial applications including automotive, defense, heavy industry, and manufacturing.
7dad7e3bc0c15d28.notes: Total raised listed as '530억원 총 누적 투자' (530 billion Korean won cumulative investment) on About page. Founders not explicitly named in provided sources. News mentions 'IPO 확률 1% 뚫었다' suggesting Pre-IPO status, but this is a speculative news headline, not verified company stage. Office locations confirmed: Seoul and Tokyo.

6e6b97caaddd3af7.notes: Source [0] is a domain parking/sales page for worldlabs.com and contains no information about a company called World Labs. The domain is listed as available for sale. No verifiable company information can be extracted from the provided sources.

c31a49738bde608d.name: nChroma Bio
c31a49738bde608d.description: Genetic medicines company developing in vivo treatments through targeted delivery of genetic cargoes designed to address unmet medical needs.
c31a49738bde608d.notes: Sources show current company name is nChroma Bio, not Chroma Medicine. Website domain is chromamedicine.com but company rebranded. Per source [1], Jeffrey Walsh joined as CEO in December 2024 following merger of Nvelop Therapeutics with Chroma Medicine. Melissa Bonner and Jenny Marlowe also joined in December 2024 post-merger. No founder names explicitly identified in provided sources. No funding amounts, valuation, or founding year specified in provided sources.
